body.title: Feeds architecture: per-device feed mailboxes + MCP send_to/view_feed tools
body.result: feeds/<device> IMAP convention: fetch_recent + send_to/send_feed/view_feed MCP tools in datacenter server + Granny publisher + GET /feeds/{device} web route + 20 tests
body.description:
Devices currently have no first-class way to publish activity streams or send directed messages without wiring up IMAPServer + Envelope directly. Introduce a 'feeds' layer built on the existing IMAP bus: per-device broadcast mailboxes at feeds/<device>, and three MCP tools on the datacenter server that any CC session can call without knowing the transport.

**Affected files:** bus/imap_server.py (create_mailbox helper), devices/granny/daemon.py (publish to feeds/granny), lab/claudecode/cc_mcp_server.py or equivalent datacenter MCP server (new tools), web service feeds route (new), tests/
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; no-TheIgors-imports; MCP tools for inter-agent comms (not CC skills — skills are for CC-side workflow; MCP tools are device-side capabilities any session can call)
**Scope boundary:** IN: feeds/<device> mailbox convention; Granny as first publisher (dispatch events + audit_fail + route_fail to feeds/granny); three MCP tools: send_to(receiver, message), send_feed(event), view_feed(sender, limit=20); web route GET /feeds/<device> returning last N events as JSON. OUT: IMAP IDLE push (use poll for now); Igor or other devices publishing feeds (follow-on tickets); UI feed reader beyond raw JSON route.
**Completion criteria:** (1) mcp__datacenter__send_to, mcp__datacenter__send_feed, mcp__datacenter__view_feed exist and are callable from a CC session. (2) Granny daemon publishes dispatch/audit_fail/route_fail events to feeds/granny. (3) GET /feeds/granny returns those events as JSON. (4) All existing Granny + bus tests pass. (5) Restart checklist: restart UU web service (new route), restart Granny daemon (new feed publishing), reload datacenter MCP server (new tools).
